title: RFE: Enable sync2jira to sync GitHub Issues to Red Hat Jira
body:
I would like to propose enabling a synchronization mechanism between the Podman GitHub repository issues and the Red Hat Jira instance using sync2jira.

Since Red Hat’s Jira is open and accessible to community members outside of Red Hat, this sync would not create a barrier for external contributors.

GitHub issues to automatically create the corresponding Jira tickets. Status updates or comments to sync using a webhook.

This applies to other projects inside the org including but not limited to Podman, Skopeo, buildah, container-libs and others.

comments.body:
> Since Red Hat’s Jira is open and accessible to community members outside of Red Hat, this sync would not create a barrier for external contributors.

Well external members should not have to open the Red Hat Jira ever, the truth must be the github issue either way.

Practically speaking this since this is optional I see no reason to object on having the label to sync.
What is more of a conversation for internally I guess is how is this supposed to be used? ? I do not see why we should track upstream activity in Jira.

For example are we supposed to mirror every issue we ever work on? So far we are being told we are not supposed to move things into a sprint after it has been started but upstream wise this just makes no sense. If I pick up an issue I want to work on it right away otherwise leave it open for others to take and then I also do not care about arbitrary sprint boundaries.

If this is for lager work items then I am not sure how much worth the mirroring is if we have to split the cards into smaller ones to be able to complete them in a sprint.
